rationale.R1.1: Positioning against prior work requires reframing the contribution claim and design-space gap above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.4: The delegation is centrally about estimating and articulating how AgentFlow differs from nearest prior systems.
rationale.R1.7: Prior-work positioning requires attention to relevant literature lines, attribution, and whether the comparison set is adequate.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must disclose uncertainty about missing named prior works and unstated evaluation context.
rationale.RX.2: The strong claim that no prior system uses this scheduling unit must be guarded against overclaiming.
rationale.RX.3: The task is limited to responsibility projection and must not execute the requested positioning work.
rationale.RX.4: Prior-work positioning can affect scholarly fairness, so harmful misattribution or exaggerated novelty must be avoided.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit prior-work framing.
